It features a thriving public art scene with numerous murals and sculptures, alongside a diverse array of restaurants, hotels, and performing arts venues that define the city's modern cultural identity Annapolis Arts District.

attractions.name: Annapolis City Dock
attractions.description: City Dock is the historic heart of downtown Annapolis, serving as a vibrant waterfront hub where the city meets the Chesapeake Bay. It is currently undergoing a massive $88 million Resiliency & Revitalization Project to address frequent flooding while transforming the area into a world-class public park and maritime destination. Known for its famous "Ego Alley" waterway, it remains a central gathering spot for boating, shopping, and dining even during its phased construction through 2028.
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 8 am - 6 pm; Fri-Sun: 8 am - 7 pm (Harbormaster Hours)
attractions.admission: Free (Docking fees: $3/foot Sun-Thu, $4/foot Fri-Sat/Holidays; 3% credit card fee)
highlights: ['Ego Alley: A famous narrow waterway for boat-watching and parading vessels.', "Kunta Kinte-Alex Haley Memorial: A sculpture group commemorating the arrival point of Alex Haley's ancestor.", 'Waterfront Park: A new, elevated public space being developed as part of the resiliency project.', 'Maritime Welcome Center: A planned visitor hub that includes the historic Burtis House.', 'Boating Access: Offers transient docking, moorings, and water taxi services.', 'Historic Market House: Located at the head of the dock, continuing a tradition of city markets since 1730.']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes for the historic cobblestone streets. Park at the Hillman Garage or use the free Magenta Shuttle from the Park Place Garage for the best experience. Early mornings are best for quiet walks along Ego Alley before the crowds arrive.
attractions.nearby: U.S. Naval Academy, St. John’s College, Maryland State House, Annapolis Maritime Museum, Paca House & Garden

attractions.name: Blackwater National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.description: Blackwater National Wildlife Refuge is a 33,000-acre sanctuary on Maryland's Eastern Shore, often referred to as the 'Everglades of the North.' Established in 1933 as a waterfowl sanctuary, it features diverse habitats including tidal marshes, pine forests, and wetlands. It is a critical stop for migratory birds on the Atlantic Flyway and supports one of the highest concentrations of nesting bald eagles on the East Coast Official Website.
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: Tuesday – Sunday, 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Closed Mondays and Federal Holidays). Outdoor Public Areas (Wildlife Drive, trails): Open daily from dawn to dusk Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (Outdoor Areas); Tuesday - Sunday (Visitor Center) Official Website

highlights: 'Wildlife Drive: A 4-mile paved scenic route for viewing waterfowl, bald eagles, and the Delmarva fox squirrel [FWS', 'Visitor Center: Features exhibits on refuge wildlife, an observation area, live Osprey and Eagle cams, and a gift shop FWS', 'Nesting Bald Eagles: Home to one of the largest breeding populations of bald eagles in the United States FWS', 'Paddling Trails: Three distinct water trails (Purple, Orange, and Green) covering 17 miles for kayaking and canoeing Visit Dorchester', 'Hiking Trails: Four land trails including the Marsh Edge Trail and Woods Trail for birding and nature photography FWS', 'Atlantic Flyway: A vital resting and feeding area for thousands of migrating ducks, geese, and swans during winter FWS']
attractions.tips: The best time to see large waterfowl populations is from mid-October to mid-March. Bring insect repellent if visiting during warmer months, as biting insects can be prevalent in the marshes. Use the official address (2145 Key Wallace Drive) in GPS rather than the refuge name to ensure you arrive at the Visitor Center. Waterproof boots are recommended for hiking trails, which can be muddy Friends of Blackwater, MidAtlantic Daytrips

attractions.name: Chase-Lloyd House (managed by Chase Home, Inc.)
attractions.description: The Chase-Lloyd House is a premier example of 18th-century Georgian architecture, originally commissioned by Samuel Chase, a signer of the Declaration of Independence. Completed by Edward Lloyd IV with interiors by master builder William Buckland, the mansion is famous for its grand cantilever staircase and elaborate Palladian window. For over 130 years, it served as a charitable boarding house for elderly women, a legacy that continues through its current stewardship Chase Home Inc., Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Church Circle (featuring St. Anne's Episcopal Church)
attractions.description: Church Circle is a historic circular plaza in downtown Annapolis, dominated by the Romanesque-Revival St. Anne's Episcopal Church. Founded in 1692, the circle is a central hub of the city's original 1694 street plan, serving as the 'hinge' between Main Street and West Street. The site remains a vibrant civic and spiritual center, housing a landmark church with deep ties to Maryland's colonial history and the city's development. Visit Annapolis, St. Anne's Episcopal Church

As Maryland's capital, it serves as a living museum of American history, showcasing Georgian architecture, maritime heritage at the City Dock, and political landmarks like the Maryland State House. attractions.name: Colonial Annapolis Historic District (Annapolis Historic District)
attractions.description: The Colonial Annapolis Historic District is a National Historic Landmark encompassing the historic core of Maryland's capital, featuring a unique 1695 Baroque street plan of circles and diagonal streets. It boasts one of the nation's highest concentrations of 18th-century Georgian architecture, including the residences of several signers of the Declaration of Independence.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Individual Museums/Sites: Generally Monday–Sunday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
attractions.admission: Free to explore the district; individual sites and museums (e.g., William Paca House, Hammond-Harwood House) typically charge $5–$15 per person for tours.
highlights: ['Baroque street plan of circles (Church Circle and State Circle) and diagonal streets.', 'Maryland State House, the oldest state capitol in continuous legislative use.', 'William Paca House & Garden, the restored home of a signer of the Declaration of Independence.', 'Charles Carroll House, the birthplace and home of the only Catholic signer of the Declaration of Independence.', 'Historic Duke of Gloucester Street, which serves as a major artery featuring significant 18th and 19th-century structures.', 'Over 120 standing 18th-century buildings, many marked with colorful historical plaques.']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Annapolis Visitors Center on West Street for maps and walking tour information. Look for the color-coded historic markers on buildings to identify different architectural periods.

attractions.name: Crypt of John Paul Jones
attractions.description: Located beneath the U.S. Naval Academy Chapel, the Crypt of John Paul Jones is the final resting place of the Revolutionary War hero known as the 'Father of the American Navy.' The site features an ornate 21-ton sarcophagus made of black and white Royal Pyrenees marble, modeled after the tomb of Napoleon.
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday: 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM (Closed on Federal Holidays and during private ceremonies) U.S. Naval Academy PAO
attractions.admission: Free (Entry to the grounds and crypt is free for those with valid identification; guided tours available for a fee) U.S. Naval Academy Tourism, Airial Travel
highlights: ['21-ton sarcophagus crafted from black and white Royal Pyrenees marble', 'Intricate bronze dolphin supports and cast sea plant embellishments', 'Brass-inlaid names of ships commanded by Jones, including the Bonhomme Richard', 'Surrounding display of historical naval artifacts and battle flags', 'Located beneath the historic U.S. Naval Academy Chapel and its Tiffany stained-glass windows', 'Architectural design by Beaux-Arts architect Whitney Warren and sculptor Sylvain Salieres']
attractions.tips: Visitors 18 and older must present a valid photo ID (REAL ID, U.S. Passport, or Enhanced Driver's License) for entry to the Academy grounds. Pedestrian entry is available through Gates 1 or 3; parking is extremely limited on-site, so using downtown Annapolis public garages is recommended. The crypt may be closed occasionally for private weddings, funerals, or memorial services held in the chapel above. U.S. Naval Academy PAO, Atlas Obscura

attractions.name: Hammond-Harwood House
attractions.description: The Hammond-Harwood House is a National Historic Landmark and a premier example of 18th-century Anglo-Palladian architecture, built in 1774 as the final work of architect William Buckland. The museum showcases an extensive collection of Maryland-made fine and decorative arts, including furniture by John Shaw and paintings by the Peale family, while interpreting the diverse social history of its free and enslaved residents. Hammond-Harwood House, Visit Annapolis

attractions.name: Herndon Monument (Commander William L. Herndon Memorial)
attractions.description: The Herndon Monument is a 21-foot-tall grey granite obelisk on the grounds of the U.S. Naval Academy, erected in 1860 to honor Commander William Lewis Herndon. It is famous as the site of the annual Herndon Monument Climb, where freshmen midshipmen (plebes) work together to scale the greased monument to replace a 'dixie cup' hat with an upperclassman's cover, symbolizing the end of their first year. U.S. Naval Academy, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. (March - December); Monday - Friday: 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m., Saturday - Sunday: 9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. (January - February) Naval Academy Tourism
attractions.days_open: Daily (except Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, New Year's Day, and Easter before noon) Naval Academy Tourism
attractions.admission: Free (Access to the Naval Academy grounds is free for pedestrians with a valid REAL ID or passport; guided tours are available for a fee) Naval Academy Tourism, U.S. Naval Academy
highlights: 'Commemorates Commander William Lewis Herndon, a hero who went down with the SS Central America in 1857 while saving passengers. [U.S. Naval Academy', "Site of the historic annual 'Herndon Climb' tradition for Naval Academy plebes. Naval Academy Tourism", 'A 21-foot-tall granite obelisk presented by the Class of 1860. attractions.tips: Visitors 18+ must have a valid REAL ID-compliant license or passport to enter the grounds; all pedestrians must enter through Gate 1. attractions.name: Historic London Town and Gardens
attractions.description: Historic London Town and Gardens is a 23-acre park featuring history, archaeology, and horticulture on the banks of the South River. It preserves the site of a "lost" 17th-century colonial port town and includes the National Historic Landmark William Brown House. Visitors can explore reconstructed colonial buildings, interactive exhibits, and 10 acres of woodland and ornamental gardens. Official Site
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 10:00am - 4:00pm, Wednesday: 10:00am - 4:00pm, Thursday: 10:00am - 4:00pm, Friday: 10:00am - 4:00pm, Saturday: 10:00am - 4:00pm, Sunday: Closed (except for special events). Official Site
attractions.admission: Members: Free; Adult: $11; Senior (62+): $10; Youth (4-17): $5; Children (3 & under): Free. Discounts: $1 off for AAA members (adult/senior); free for care partners of visitors with disabilities; free for up to 4 people via Museums for All (EBT card). Official Site
highlights: ['William Brown House: A circa 1760 Georgian brick tavern and National Historic Landmark.', "Reconstructed Buildings: Explore the Carpenter's Shop and Lord Mayor's Tenement built on original archaeological footprints.", 'Botanical Gardens: 10 acres of woodland and ornamental gardens including the Azalea Glade and Camellia Walk.', 'Sound and Sensory Garden: An interactive natural play area for children featuring musical instruments.', 'Active Archaeology: Site of the Lost Towns Project, an ongoing investigation into the colonial settlement.', 'UNESCO Site of Memory: Recognized for its history as an early port in the slave trade.']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the modern Visitor Center to view the "Discover London Town" exhibit and join a guided tour, which is the only way to access the historic interiors. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the grounds feature gravel paths and the historic house has steep stairs. Leashed pets are welcome in the gardens and buildings except for the William Brown House. Official Site

attractions.name: James Brice House Historic Annapolis
attractions.category: Museum, Landmark, Historic House Historic Annapolis
attractions.description: The James Brice House is one of the largest and most elegant of Annapolis’s historic homes and a National Historic Landmark. Built between 1767 and 1774, it is a magnificent five-part Georgian mansion currently undergoing a multi-year, multi-million dollar "museum-grade" restoration to return it to its 1774 appearance (Historic Annapolis).
attractions.address: 42 East Street, Annapolis, MD 21401 Historic Annapolis
attractions.hours: Closed to the public for restoration; call for group tour availability (Historic Annapolis)
attractions.days_open: Closed for general admission; group tours by appointment only (Historic Annapolis)
attractions.admission: Currently closed to the public for restoration; group tours are available by appointment by calling 410-267-7619 (Historic Annapolis)
highlights: 'One of the most important surviving structures from colonial America ([Historic Annapolis)', "Extremely detailed construction records exist in James Brice's original account book (Historic Annapolis)", 'Exceptional architectural details including a mahogany staircase and plaster cornices (Visit Annapolis)', 'Discovery of "Hoodoo caches" under the floors, reflecting African-American spiritual beliefs (Archaeology in Annapolis)', 'Currently the site of an ambitious "preservation project of our lifetime" restoration (Historic Annapolis)']
attractions.tips: The house is currently a "hard hat site" with restricted access, though the exterior remains in excellent condition for viewing from the street (Tripadvisor). Special "behind-the-scenes" restoration tours are periodically offered (Historic Annapolis). It is also a featured stop on local ghost tours due to its reputation as a haunted site (US Ghost Adventures).

attractions.name: Maryland State House
attractions.description: The Maryland State House is the oldest state capitol in continuous legislative use in the United States, completed in 1779. It holds significant American history, having served as the nation's capitol from 1783 to 1784 and witnessing George Washington's resignation as commander-in-chief. Today, it remains the seat of the Maryland General Assembly and the offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor (Maryland State Archives).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:30 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on Christmas and New Year's Day) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'Oldest state capitol in continuous legislative use ([National Park Service)', 'Old Senate Chamber where George Washington resigned his commission (Official Website)', 'The largest wooden dome in the United States constructed without nails (Tripadvisor)', 'Site of the ratification of the Treaty of Paris, ending the Revolutionary War (Maryland State Archives)', 'Maryland General Assembly chambers for the House of Delegates and Senate (Maryland.gov)', 'Extensive collection of historic artwork and memorials (Maryland State Archives)']
attractions.tips: A valid photo ID is required for entry for all adults, and visitors must pass through security screenings including metal detectors. Self-guided tour brochures are available in the Office of Interpretation on the first floor to help navigate the historic rooms. Group tours for educational programs should be reserved at least two weeks in advance (Maryland State Archives).

The venue is also home to the world-famous Rams Head On Stage, an intimate concert hall that hosts over 300 shows annually.

attractions.description: Spa Creek is a picturesque tributary of the Severn River that winds through the heart of historic Annapolis and the maritime neighborhood of Eastport. It serves as a vital recreational and ecological resource, offering scenic waterfront views, boat access, and restored headwater trails for hikers and nature enthusiasts. (Spa Creek Conservancy, Visit Annapolis)

(Spa Creek Conservancy, Watermark)

attractions.name: Spa Creek Drawbridge
attractions.description: The Spa Creek Drawbridge is a historic bascule bridge, constructed in 1946, that serves as a vital link between downtown Annapolis and the Eastport neighborhood. It is a popular destination for pedestrians who enjoy panoramic views of the Annapolis harbor, "Ego Alley," and the United States Naval Academy.

Nov 1–Apr 30: Mon–Fri closed 7:30–9 AM & 4:30–6 PM; opens on signal at other times. Weekends/Holidays: Opens on the hour and half-hour year-round.
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Historic 1946 bascule (drawbridge) design.', 'Provides a scenic pedestrian connection between the Historic District and Eastport.', 'Prime viewing spot for the Wednesday night sailboat races.', 'Offers wide sidewalks on both sides for photography and sightseeing.', 'Watch the mechanical lift as large yachts and sailboats pass through to the harbor.', 'Centerpiece of local traditions, including the "Republic of Eastport" bridge tug-of-war.']
attractions.tips: Visit on a Wednesday evening to watch sailboats return from races, and time your crossing for the top or bottom of the hour to witness the bridge operation. Bring a camera for some of the best skyline shots of the city.

attractions.name: Westgate Circle
attractions.description: Westgate Circle is a prominent elliptical traffic roundabout and public art space that serves as the symbolic gateway from modern Annapolis into the Historic District. Completed in 1999 as part of a multi-million dollar revitalization project, it anchors the city's Arts District and features a central landscaped island designed to host major public sculptures. This key intersection connects West Street, Spa Road, and Taylor Avenue, acting as a focal point for the surrounding mix of hotels, businesses, and residential communities.
attractions.address: West Street at Spa Road and Taylor Avenue, Annapolis, MD 21401
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily
attractions.admission: Free

attractions.tips: The circle is best viewed on foot while walking toward downtown; use the designated brick-paved crosswalks to navigate the busy intersection. Be sure to check for any new public art installations in the central island, as the city frequently commissions new focal pieces for the site.

attractions.name: William Paca House and Garden
attractions.description: The William Paca House and Garden is a meticulously restored 18th-century Georgian mansion and two-acre wilderness garden located in the heart of the Annapolis Historic District. Built in the 1760s for William Paca, a signer of the Declaration of Independence and Maryland's third governor, the site is recognized as a National Historic Landmark. Visitors can explore the elegant interiors via guided tours and stroll through a picturesque garden featuring formal parterres, a spring-fed pond, and a reconstructed summerhouse. Historic Annapolis, National Park Service
attractions.hours: Sunday - Thursday: 10 am – 5 pm; Friday & Saturday: 10 am – 3 pm (Summer Hours). Historic Annapolis
attractions.days_open: Daily (seasonal variations apply; currently open Sunday through Saturday). Historic Annapolis

Visitors can explore high-tech exhibits, watch the 'Call to Serve' film, and join guided tours of the historic campus known as 'The Yard' (Naval Academy Tourism, Visit Annapolis).

Named after Navy founder George Bancroft, the massive Beaux-Arts style complex features over 1,700 rooms and nearly five miles of corridors, functioning as a self-contained city for midshipmen. It features a sandy beach for cartop boat launching, a 288-foot fishing pier converted from a section of the old Naval Academy Bridge, and offers spectacular views of the Naval Academy and sunsets over the water (Anne Arundel County, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: United States Naval Academy Chapel
attractions.description: The United States Naval Academy Chapel, known as the 'Cathedral of the Navy,' is a historic Beaux-Arts landmark and the architectural centerpiece of the Academy's grounds. Featuring a grand copper-clad dome and housing the crypt of Revolutionary War hero John Paul Jones, it serves as a spiritual and symbolic heart for the Brigade of Midshipmen. U.S. Naval Institute, Visit Annapolis

[USNA Tourism, Navyonline']

Located in historic Preble Hall, it features a vast collection of artifacts, including world-class ship models and historic flags, using interactive technology to bring maritime history to life (Naval Academy Tourism). The museum serves to educate midshipmen and the public on the contributions of Naval Academy graduates to national history (Naval Academy Tourism).

attractions.name: West Annapolis
attractions.description: West Annapolis is a charming waterfront neighborhood and business district nestled along the Severn River, just minutes from downtown Annapolis. Known for its village-like atmosphere, it features a collection of colorful boutiques, antique stores, upscale professional services, and local cafes. The community offers a serene escape with coastal tranquility and is a popular destination for those seeking unique shopping and dining experiences outside the historic center.

It is famously known as the 'Maritime Republic of Eastport,' a tongue-in-cheek micronation declared by residents in 1998 to promote local businesses. This quirky, walkable community features narrow streets lined with cottages, working marinas, and vibrant street-end parks offering stunning harbor views.

highlights: ['Kenneth R. Dunn Municipal Pool (seasonal)', "Roger 'Pip' Moyer Recreation Center with fitness center and rock wall", 'Public boat ramp and launch area for kayaks and canoes on Spa Creek', 'Tennis and pickleball courts (2025 Public Park of the Year)', 'Skateboard park and multiple sports fields (Collison and Griscom)', 'Scenic hiking and walking trails overlooking the water']

attractions.name: Heritage Harbour Community Association
attractions.description: Heritage Harbour is a premier 55+ active adult community located in Anne Arundel County near historic Annapolis. The community features over 1,600 residences including single-family homes, villas, and condos, all centered around an extensive array of recreational facilities. Residents enjoy a vibrant lifestyle with access to a large community lodge, multiple pools, and a variety of social clubs and activities. Heritage Harbour
attractions.hours: Lodge Office: Weekdays 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Tours by appointment); Facility hours vary by activity Heritage Harbour
attractions.admission: Private Community (Annual Assessment: $2,112 in 2026; facility access included for residents) Heritage Harbour
highlights: ['Community Lodge with atrium, auditorium, and library', 'Indoor and outdoor swimming pools with water aerobics programs', 'Nine-hole golf course and pro shop', 'South River waterfront pier with boat slips and kayak storage', 'Tennis and pickleball courts for all skill levels', 'Organized community bus trips to shopping and cultural destinations']
attractions.tips: Tours of the Lodge and facilities must be booked in advance through the HHCA office. Potential buyers should note that many amenities are reserved exclusively for residents and their guests. Heritage Harbour
attractions.nearby: United States Naval Academy, Historic Annapolis District, South River, Annapolis Town Center Visit Maryland

attractions.name: Sandy Point State Park
attractions.description: Sandy Point State Park is a 786-acre recreational area located on the northwestern shore of the Chesapeake Bay. It offers breathtaking scenic water views, a mile-long sandy beach, and extensive facilities for swimming, fishing, and boating. Maryland DNR, Visit Maryland
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 a.m. to sunset (year-round, except Christmas Day); Fishing and Boating: 24-hour access; Winter Exception (Mid-Nov to Jan 2): 7:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. due to Lights on the Bay. Maryland DNR
attractions.admission: May 1 - September 30 (Weekends/Holidays): $5/person; May 1 - September 30 (Weekdays): $4/person; October 1 - April 30: $3/vehicle; Free for Maryland Park Service Season Passport holders Maryland DNR
highlights: ['One-mile public swimming beach with views of the Chesapeake Bay Bridge', 'Marina with 22 public boat ramps and small craft launch area', 'Sandy Point Nature Center featuring an aquarium and interpretive programs', 'Historic Sandy Point Mansion and views of the Sandy Point Shoal Lighthouse', 'Fishing and crabbing piers available 24 hours a day', 'Corcoran Environmental Study Area with 4 miles of additional hiking trails']
attractions.tips: The park often reaches capacity on summer weekends; arrive early and consider making required day-use reservations. Pets are allowed on the beach only from October 1 through April 30. Alcohol and glass containers are prohibited on the sandy beach. Maryland DNR
attractions.nearby: Chesapeake Bay Bridge, Annapolis City Dock, US Naval Academy, William Paca House & Garden, Annapolis Maritime Museum & Park Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Severn River
attractions.description: The Severn River is a picturesque 14-mile-long tidal estuary in Anne Arundel County, Maryland, flowing into the Chesapeake Bay at Annapolis. It is a designated Maryland Scenic River, famous for hosting the United States Naval Academy at its mouth and offering extensive opportunities for boating, fishing, and historical exploration. Wikipedia), Severn River Association
attractions.address: 2001 Baltimore Annapolis Blvd (Jonas Green Park Access)
attractions.hours: Dawn to Sunset (Parks); 24/7 for boating access Anne Arundel County
attractions.days_open: 7 days a week Anne Arundel County
attractions.admission: Free (Public access at Jonas Green Park and other points; some nearby state parks may charge $3-$5) Maryland DNR
highlights: "Home to the United States Naval Academy at the river's mouth [Wikipedia)", "Designated as one of Maryland's official Scenic Rivers Anne Arundel County", 'Popular destination for sailing, kayaking, and powerboating Severn Riverkeeper', 'Site of the 1655 Battle of the Severn and the colonial settlement of Providence Wikipedia)', 'Extensive public access through parks like Jonas Green Park and Severn Run Natural Environment Area Anne Arundel County', "Vibrant ecosystem supported by the Severn River Association's restoration projects Severn River Association"]
attractions.tips: Visit Jonas Green Park for free fishing and car-top boat launching with views of the Naval Academy. Always check the Navy's schedule for range operations if planning to hike at Greenbury Point. Most shoreline beaches are private community property, so use public access points. Round Bay is a favorite spot for boaters to anchor and watch the sunset. PropTalk, Navy.mil
attractions.nearby: U.S. Naval Academy, Annapolis City Dock (Ego Alley), Maryland State House, Sandy Point State Park, Annapolis Maritime Museum Visit Maryland

attractions.name: Truxton Heights
attractions.description: Truxton Heights is a residential neighborhood in Annapolis, Maryland, situated along the scenic Spa Creek. The community is best known for being the home of Truxtun Park, an 80-acre recreational hub offering boat access, hiking trails, and the city's primary recreation center.
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 Hours, Tuesday: Open 24 Hours, Wednesday: Open 24 Hours, Thursday: Open 24 Hours, Friday: Open 24 Hours, Saturday: Open 24 Hours, Sunday: Open 24 Hours (Note: Park amenities typically operate Sunrise to Sunset)
attractions.admission: Free for neighborhood and park access; Pip Moyer Recreation Center daily rates: Adult $8 ($10 non-resident), Senior $7 ($8 non-resident), Youth $6 ($7 non-resident); Boat ramp: $10 daily launch fee.
highlights: ['Proximity to the 80-acre Truxtun Park and its extensive trail system', 'Public boat ramp access to Spa Creek with finger piers and launching ramps', 'Roger "Pip" Moyer Recreation Center, featuring a fitness center and rock wall', 'Kenneth R. Dunn Municipal Pool and seasonal splash park facilities', 'Strategic location in Ward 5 with convenient access to downtown Annapolis', 'Ongoing city infrastructure projects for enhanced pedestrian safety and drainage']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to park at the Pip Moyer Recreation Center for easy access to the neighborhood's trails and playgrounds. Check the seasonal schedule for the Kenneth R. Dunn Municipal Pool, which usually operates from late May through Labor Day.
attractions.nearby: Downtown Historic Annapolis, Spa Creek, Annapolis Maritime Museum & Park, Quiet Waters Park.

attractions.name: William Preston Lane Jr. Memorial (Chesapeake Bay) Bridge
attractions.description: The Chesapeake Bay Bridge, officially the William Preston Lane Jr. Memorial Bridge, is a major dual-span bridge in Maryland connecting the state's Eastern Shore with the Western Shore. Spanning approximately 4.3 miles, it is one of the world's longest and most scenic over-water steel structures, serving as a vital link for U.S. Routes 50 and 301.
attractions.hours: 24/7 (Always Open)
attractions.admission: $2.50 (E-ZPass Maryland), $4.00 (E-ZPass Out-of-State / Pay-By-Plate), $6.00 (Video Toll); Tolls collected Eastbound only. Free for Westbound traffic.
highlights: ['Dual-span suspension bridge spanning 4.3 miles across the Chesapeake Bay.', 'A vital transportation link connecting the Annapolis area to the Eastern Shore and Ocean City.', 'Features all-electronic (cashless) tolling for a smoother travel experience.', 'Offers stunning scenic views of the bay, sailboats, and local lighthouses.', 'Includes an original span opened in 1952 and a parallel span added in 1973.', 'Recognized as a historic landmark eligible for the National Register of Historic Places.']
attractions.tips: Check real-time traffic updates via 1-877-BAYSPAN before traveling, as the bridge is prone to heavy congestion during summer weekends. Note that tolls are only collected in the eastbound direction, and the bridge uses a cashless system, so ensure your E-ZPass is active or be prepared for a video toll bill. Use the 'Best Times to Travel' guide on the official website to avoid peak delays.
attractions.nearby: Sandy Point State Park, Thomas Point Shoal Lighthouse, United States Naval Academy, Historic Downtown Annapolis, Kent Island.

attractions.name: Sherwood Gardens (Official Website)
attractions.description: Sherwood Gardens is a historic six-acre private park located in the Guilford neighborhood of Northern Baltimore, renowned for its spectacular annual tulip display. Established in the 1920s by John W. Sherwood, the gardens feature nearly 80,000 imported tulips alongside rare trees and flowering shrubs within an Olmsted-designed landscape. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Dawn to Dusk (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Every day of the year (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations are encouraged) (Official Website)
highlights: 'Annual spring display of approximately 80,000 tulips ([Official Website)', 'Level I accredited arboretum featuring rare and majestic trees (Official Website)', 'Olmsted-designed pastoral landscape with no gates or fences (Official Website)', 'Seasonal blooms including azaleas, dogwoods, magnolias, and summer perennials (Official Website)', "Annual 'Tulip Dig' on Memorial Day weekend where visitors can purchase bulbs (Wikipedia)"]
attractions.tips: Peak tulip bloom typically occurs from late April to early May. Note that there are no public restrooms or trash receptacles on-site, and visitors must utilize two-hour residential street parking. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Baltimore Museum of Art, Johns Hopkins University, Homewood Museum, Stony Run Park (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
